message:
feat(primitives): expand LAX primitive set with tensor-aware AD, new math ops, axis-aware reductions, and select

This commit significantly extends the FrankenJAX primitive algebra across
six crates to close critical gaps that blocked tensor-level automatic
differentiation and real-world JAX program coverage.

## Why these changes were needed

The prior primitive set covered only basic arithmetic (Add, Sub, Mul, Neg),
a handful of transcendentals (Sin, Cos, Exp, Log, Sqrt, Pow), and simple
reductions. This was insufficient for three reasons:

1. **Missing math primitives**: JAX programs routinely use Tan, Asin, Acos,
   Atan, Sinh, Cosh, Tanh, Expm1, Log1p, Sign, Square, Reciprocal,
   Logistic (sigmoid), Erf, Erfc, Div, Rem, Atan2, and Select. Without
   these, tracing any non-trivial JAX program would fail at dispatch.

2. **Scalar-only AD backward pass**: The reverse-mode VJP (backward) pass
   operated entirely on f64 scalars, making it impossible to differentiate
   programs that operate on tensors. The cotangent accumulation, seed
   construction, and all VJP rules needed to be lifted to work on Value
   (which can be either Scalar or Tensor).

3. **No axis-aware reductions**: ReduceSum/Max/Min/Prod previously
   collapsed tensors to scalars unconditionally. JAX's reduce operations
   accept an `axes` parameter for partial reduction (e.g., summing along
   one axis of a matrix to produce a vector). The VJP of ReduceSum also
   needs to broadcast the cotangent back to the input shape, which
   requires knowing which axes were reduced.

## What changed, crate by crate

### fj-core (Primitive enum + proptest strategies)
- Added 19 new Primitive variants: Tan, Asin, Acos, Atan, Sinh, Cosh,
  Tanh, Expm1, Log1p, Sign, Square, Reciprocal, Logistic, Erf, Erfc,
  Div, Rem, Atan2, Select.
- Extended `as_str()` mappings for canonical fingerprinting.
- Updated `arb_primitive()` proptest strategy to cover all new variants.

### fj-lax (evaluation engine)
- Wired all 19 new primitives through `eval_primitive` to the appropriate
  elementwise/binary/unary evaluators.
- Added `eval_select()` for element-wise ternary select (bool condition).
- Added `erf_approx()` using Abramowitz & Stegun (max error ~1.5e-7),
  since Rust's standard library does not provide erf/erfc.
- Introduced `eval_reduce_axes()` which parses an optional `axes` param
  from the equation params map and performs partial reduction along
  specified axes, computing output shape by removing reduced dimensions.
  When no axes are specified, falls back to full reduction for backward
  compatibility.
- Added helper functions `compute_strides`, `flat_to_multi`, and
  `multi_to_out_flat` for correct N-dimensional index arithmetic during
  partial reduction.

### fj-ad (automatic differentiation)
- **Tensor-aware backward pass**: The `backward()` function now threads
  `Value` cotangents instead of `f64`. Adjoint accumulation uses
  `value_add` (dispatched through eval_primitive) so tensor + tensor
  gradients are handled correctly.
- **Tensor-aware VJP rules**: Every VJP rule was rewritten to operate on
  `Value` references and delegate arithmetic to eval_primitive calls
  (value_mul, value_neg, value_div, value_sub, value_add). This means
  the backward pass works identically for scalar and tensor inputs.
- Added VJP rules for all 19 new primitives, with correct mathematical
  derivatives (e.g., d/dx tan(x) = 1/cos^2(x), d/dx logistic(x) =
  sigma(x)(1-sigma(x)), d/dx erf(x) = 2/sqrt(pi)*exp(-x^2), etc.).
- Added VJP rules for shape-manipulation ops (Reshape, Transpose,
  BroadcastInDim, Slice, Concatenate) that were previously unsupported.
  Reshape VJP reshapes g back to original shape; Transpose VJP applies
  inverse permutation; BroadcastInDim VJP reduces summed broadcast dims.
- `TapeEntry` now stores `params` so VJP rules for parameterized ops
  (Transpose permutation, Reshape target shape) can access them.
- `grad_jaxpr()` now returns `Vec<Value>` instead of `Vec<f64>`, enabling
  tensor gradient outputs.
- `grad_first()` remains a convenience wrapper returning `f64` for
  backward compatibility.
- Added helper functions: `zeros_like`, `ones_like`, `scalar_value`,
  `is_zero_value`, `broadcast_g_to_shape` for tensor AD plumbing.
- JVP (forward-mode) rules extended for all new primitives with correct
  scalar tangent propagation.
- Added unit tests: grad_tan, grad_tanh, grad_logistic, grad_square,
  grad_reciprocal, grad_div confirming correct derivatives.
- Added `make_unary_jaxpr` / `make_binary_jaxpr` test helpers.

### fj-dispatch (transform execution)
- `execute_grad` no longer requires the first argument to be scalar when
  using symbolic AD (only finite-diff fallback needs scalar inputs).
- Returns `Value` gradient from `grad_jaxpr` directly instead of
  extracting a scalar, enabling tensor gradient dispatch.

### fj-trace (tracing / shape inference)
- Added shape inference rules for all 19 new primitives:
  - Unary: Tan, Asin, Acos, Atan, Sinh, Cosh, Tanh, Expm1, Log1p,
    Sign, Square, Reciprocal, Logistic, Erf, Erfc (shape-preserving).
  - Binary: Div, Rem, Atan2 (broadcast shapes like Add/Mul).
  - Ternary: Select (broadcast all three inputs, dtype from on_true).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

     Sin,

+    Erfc,
+    // Binary math

     Dot,
     // Comparison

@@ -9,9 +9,12 @@ mod type_promotion;
 use fj_core::{Primitive, Shape, Value, ValueError};
 use std::collections::BTreeMap;
 
-use arithmetic::{eval_binary_elementwise, eval_dot, eval_unary_elementwise, eval_unary_int_or_float};
+use arithmetic::{
+    eval_binary_elementwise, eval_dot, eval_select, eval_unary_elementwise,
+    eval_unary_int_or_float, erf_approx,
+};
 use comparison::eval_comparison;
-use reduction::eval_reduce;
+use reduction::{eval_reduce, eval_reduce_axes};
 use tensor_ops::{eval_broadcast_in_dim, eval_concatenate, eval_reshape, eval_slice, eval_transpose};
 
 #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
@@ -117,6 +120,54 @@ pub fn eval_primitive(
         // Trigonometric
         Primitive::Sin => eval_unary_elementwise(primitive, inputs, f64::sin),
         Primitive::Cos => eval_unary_elementwise(primitive, inputs, f64::cos),
+        Primitive::Tan => eval_unary_elementwise(primitive, inputs, f64::tan),
+        Primitive::Asin => eval_unary_elementwise(primitive, inputs, f64::asin),
+        Primitive::Acos => eval_unary_elementwise(primitive, inputs, f64::acos),
+        Primitive::Atan => eval_unary_elementwise(primitive, inputs, f64::atan),
+        // Hyperbolic
+        Primitive::Sinh => eval_unary_elementwise(primitive, inputs, f64::sinh),
+        Primitive::Cosh => eval_unary_elementwise(primitive, inputs, f64::cosh),
+        Primitive::Tanh => eval_unary_elementwise(primitive, inputs, f64::tanh),
+        // Additional math
+        Primitive::Expm1 => eval_unary_elementwise(primitive, inputs, f64::exp_m1),
+        Primitive::Log1p => eval_unary_elementwise(primitive, inputs, f64::ln_1p),
+        Primitive::Sign => eval_unary_int_or_float(
+            primitive,
+            inputs,
+            |x| x.signum(),
+            |x| {
+                if x.is_nan() {
+                    f64::NAN
+                } else {
+                    x.signum()
+                }
+            },
+        ),
+        Primitive::Square => eval_unary_int_or_float(primitive, inputs, |x| x * x, |x| x * x),
+        Primitive::Reciprocal => eval_unary_elementwise(primitive, inputs, |x| 1.0 / x),
+        Primitive::Logistic => {
+            eval_unary_elementwise(primitive, inputs, |x| 1.0 / (1.0 + (-x).exp()))
+        }
+        Primitive::Erf => eval_unary_elementwise(primitive, inputs, erf_approx),
+        Primitive::Erfc => eval_unary_elementwise(primitive, inputs, |x| 1.0 - erf_approx(x)),
+        // Binary math
+        Primitive::Div => eval_binary_elementwise(
+            primitive,
+            inputs,
+            |a, b| if b != 0 { a / b } else { 0 },
+            |a, b| a / b,
+        ),
+        Primitive::Rem => eval_binary_elementwise(
+            primitive,
+            inputs,
+            |a, b| if b != 0 { a % b } else { 0 },
+            |a, b| a % b,
+        ),
+        Primitive::Atan2 => {
+            eval_binary_elementwise(primitive, inputs, |a, b| (a as f64).atan2(b as f64) as i64, f64::atan2)
+        }
+        // Selection
+        Primitive::Select => eval_select(primitive, inputs),
         // Dot product
         Primitive::Dot => eval_dot(inputs),
         // Comparison
@@ -126,28 +177,30 @@ pub fn eval_primitive(
         Primitive::Le => eval_comparison(primitive, inputs, |a, b| a <= b, |a, b| a <= b),
         Primitive::Gt => eval_comparison(primitive, inputs, |a, b| a > b, |a, b| a > b),
         Primitive::Ge => eval_comparison(primitive, inputs, |a, b| a >= b, |a, b| a >= b),
-        // Reductions
+        // Reductions (axis-aware)
         Primitive::ReduceSum => {
-            eval_reduce(primitive, inputs, 0_i64, 0.0, |a, b| a + b, |a, b| a + b)
+            eval_reduce_axes(primitive, inputs, params, 0_i64, 0.0, |a, b| a + b, |a, b| a + b)
         }
-        Primitive::ReduceMax => eval_reduce(
+        Primitive::ReduceMax => eval_reduce_axes(
             primitive,
             inputs,
+            params,
             i64::MIN,
             f64::NEG_INFINITY,
             i64::max,
             f64::max,
         ),
-        Primitive::ReduceMin => eval_reduce(
+        Primitive::ReduceMin => eval_reduce_axes(
             primitive,
             inputs,
+            params,
             i64::MAX,
             f64::INFINITY,
             i64::min,
             f64::min,
         ),
         Primitive::ReduceProd => {
-            eval_reduce(primitive, inputs, 1_i64, 1.0, |a, b| a * b, |a, b| a * b)
+            eval_reduce_axes(primitive, inputs, params, 1_i64, 1.0, |a, b| a * b, |a, b| a * b)
         }
         // Shape manipulation
         Primitive::Reshape => eval_reshape(inputs, params),
